narration_text: Now we'll see how the various components of the internet come together. Notice that the internet can be thought of as having an edge, where our devices and access networks reside, and a core, where packet switching takes place. Look at the diagram on your slide — notice how the different layers and components are interconnected, with physical media, such as cables and wireless links, supporting the network. I want you to focus on how the image illustrates the relationship between the network edge and core, and how this structure enables data to be transmitted efficiently. As we move forward, let's keep in mind how these concepts fit into the broader context of internet architecture.
